story.iWant: a diagnostics queue for batching telemetry events and a tool interceptor chain for blocking pre/post transformations on MCP tool calls
story.soThat: I can collect telemetry from providers and MCP tool calls with zero hot-path overhead, route diagnostics to the cost tracker, and apply sanitization/validation interceptors to tool invocations without modifying core tool execution logic

task.title: Create DiagnosticsEvent types and DiagnosticsQueue class

tasks.task: - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-event.ts` with discriminated union `DiagnosticsEvent` type (F01) - [ ] Import `AgentType` from `../types/knowledge.js` - [ ] Define 6 event types: `tool:invoke`, `tool:complete`, `tool:error`, `provider:call`, `provider:complete`, `provider:error` - [ ] Define `DiagnosticsErrorCode` union type (F01) - [ ] Define `DiagnosticsEventBase` with shared fields, `correlationId?: string` (F15), and optional context fields - [ ] Define `ToolDiagnosticsEvent extends DiagnosticsEventBase` with required `toolName` (F01) - [ ] Define `ProviderDiagnosticsEvent extends DiagnosticsEventBase` with required `providerName` (F01) - [ ] Export `DiagnosticsEvent = ToolDiagnosticsEvent | ProviderDiagnosticsEvent` (F01) - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-queue.ts` with `DiagnosticsQueue` class implementing `IDiagnosticsQueue` (F18) - [ ] Export `DiagnosticsEventProcessor` type alias, `DiagnosticsQueueLogger` interface, `IDiagnosticsQueue` interface (F02, F18) - [ ] Constructor accepts `{ drainIntervalMs?, maxQueueSize?, logger?: DiagnosticsQueueLogger }` (F02) - [ ] Implement `emit()` as synchronous push with bounded queue (drop oldest when full), increment `droppedCount` (F02) - [ ] Implement `getDroppedCount()` accessor (F02) - [ ] Implement timer-based `drain()` with `drainPromise` guard and structured error logging (F05) - [ ] Implement `setProcessor()` that starts the drain timer with `.unref()` - [ ] Implement `dispose()` that clears timer then re-drains in loop until empty (max 10 iterations) (F12) - [ ] Events MUST be delivered to processors in FIFO order; batching preserves insertion order (F17) - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/telemetry/index.ts` barrel export - [ ] MODIFY `packages/shared/package.json` -- add `./telemetry` entry to the exports map (F13)

task.title: Create DiagnosticsProcessor mapping events to cost tracker

tasks.task: - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-processor.ts` - [ ] Import `ICostTracker`, `UsageRecordInput` from `@tamma/cost-monitor`; use `mapProviderName()` and `mapTaskType()` instead of unsafe casts (F08) - [ ] Import `ILogger` from `../contracts.js` - [ ] Implement `createDiagnosticsProcessor()` factory accepting `ICostTracker` and optional `ILogger` - [ ] Filter to only `tool:complete`, `tool:error`, `provider:complete`, `provider:error` events - [ ] Map `DiagnosticsEvent` fields to `UsageRecordInput` per mapping table (F19) - [ ] Swallow per-event errors with optional logger warning - [ ] Define `IDiagnosticsProcessor` interface in `@tamma/shared` to avoid circular deps; implement concrete processor at app level (F03) - [ ] Prerequisite: `@tamma/cost-monitor` must import `AgentType` from `@tamma/shared`, not redefine locally (F20) - [ ] Modify `packages/shared/src/index.ts` to export telemetry barrel

- [ ] Create `packages/mcp-client/src/interceptors.ts` - [ ] Import `ToolResult` from `./types.js` - [ ] Define `PreInterceptor` type: `(toolName, args) => Promise<{ args, warnings }>` - [ ] Define `PostInterceptor` type: `(toolName, result) => Promise<{ result, warnings }>` - [ ] Implement `ToolInterceptorChain` class with `addPreInterceptor()`, `addPostInterceptor()` - [ ] Implement `runPre()` with try/catch per interceptor (fail-open for non-security); validate returned args for prototype pollution keys (`__proto__`, `constructor`, `prototype`) (F09, F16) - [ ] Implement `runPost()` with try/catch per interceptor (fail-open for non-security) (F09) - [ ] Empty chain must be a no-op passthrough

- [ ] Use `IContentSanitizer` (I-prefix) from Story 9-7, which returns `{ result: string; warnings: string[] }` -- remove local `ContentSanitizer` interface (F10) - [ ] Use `validateUrl()` from Story 9-7 which returns `{ valid: boolean; warnings: string[] }` -- remove separate `UrlValidator` interface (F11) - [ ] Implement `createSanitizationInterceptor(sanitizer: IContentSanitizer)` returning a `PostInterceptor` that sanitizes text content in `ToolResult` - [ ] Implement `createUrlValidationInterceptor(validateUrl)` returning a `PreInterceptor` that validates URL args - [ ] Export all types and factories from `packages/mcp-client/src/interceptors.ts` - [ ] Update `packages/mcp-client/src/index.ts` to export interceptors

task.title: Modify MCPClient.invokeTool() for interceptor chain support

tasks.task: - [ ] Add `private interceptorChain?: ToolInterceptorChain` field to `MCPClient` - [ ] Preferred: accept interceptor chain in `MCPClientOptions` at construction time; alternatively add `setInterceptorChain()` setter (F04) - [ ] In `invokeTool()`, after argument validation and before `tool:invoked` emit, run `interceptorChain.runPre()` to transform args - [ ] Log pre-interceptor warnings via `this.logger?.warn()` (F14) - [ ] In `invokeTool()`, after result construction and before `tool:completed` emit, run `interceptorChain.runPost()` to transform result - [ ] Log post-interceptor warnings via `this.logger?.warn()` (F14) - [ ] Use `finalArgs` from pre-interceptor output for execution - [ ] Use `finalResult` from post-interceptor output for return value

- [ ] Document the wiring pattern: `mcpClient.on('tool:invoked', ...)` maps to `diagnosticsQueue.emit({ type: 'tool:invoke', ... })` - [ ] Document the wiring pattern: `mcpClient.on('tool:completed', ...)` maps to `diagnosticsQueue.emit({ type: data.success ? 'tool:complete' : 'tool:error', ... })` - [ ] Generate `correlationId` via `crypto.randomUUID()` for start/end event pairing (F15) - [ ] Truncate args via `truncateArgs()` (max 10KB) before queueing (F06) - [ ] Redact sensitive keys (password, token, apiKey, secret, authorization) from args before queueing (F07) - [ ] Sanitize `errorMessage` via `sanitizeErrorMessage()` from Story 9-2 before storing (F07) - [ ] Context fields (agentType, projectId, engineId, taskId, taskType) come from the caller's runtime context - [ ] Create unit tests for the bridge wiring logic - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-queue.test.ts` - [ ] Create `packages/shared/src/telemetry/diagnostics-processor.test.ts` - [ ] Create `packages/mcp-client/src/interceptors.test.ts`

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `DiagnosticsEvent` is a discriminated union of `ToolDiagnosticsEvent` and `ProviderDiagnosticsEvent` with typed `DiagnosticsErrorCode` (F01)
`DiagnosticsQueue.emit()` is synchronous -- returns before queue drains
Queue drains to processor on interval (5000ms default)
`drainPromise` guard prevents concurrent drain from timer + dispose racing
Processor errors are logged via `DiagnosticsQueueLogger.warn()` (not swallowed silently) (F05)
Queue drops oldest events when full (`maxQueueSize`, default 1000) and increments `droppedCount` (F02)
`dispose()` stops timer then re-drains in loop until empty (max 10 iterations) (F12)
Drain timer uses `.unref()` so it does not keep the process alive
`createDiagnosticsProcessor()` maps `DiagnosticsEvent` to `UsageRecordInput` using `mapProviderName()` and `mapTaskType()` (F08)
Pre-interceptor modifies args (blocking, awaited)
Post-interceptor sanitizes result (blocking, awaited)
Interceptors run in registration order, piped (output of one is input to next)
Empty interceptor chain is a no-op passthrough
`createSanitizationInterceptor()` uses `IContentSanitizer` from Story 9-7 (F10)
`createUrlValidationInterceptor()` uses `validateUrl()` from Story 9-7 (F11)
`invokeTool()` calls `runPre()` before execution and `runPost()` after, and logs warnings (F14)
Interceptor chain can be set via `MCPClientOptions` or `setInterceptorChain()` (F04)
Bridge wiring maps `mcpClient` events to `diagnosticsQueue.emit()` with `correlationId`, truncated args, and redacted sensitive keys (F06, F07, F15)
`pnpm --filter @tamma/shared run typecheck` passes
`pnpm vitest run packages/shared/src/telemetry` passes
`pnpm vitest run packages/mcp-client/src/interceptors` passes
Events are delivered to processors in FIFO order; batching preserves insertion order (F17)
`IDiagnosticsQueue` interface exported with `emit()`, `setProcessor()`, `dispose()`, `getDroppedCount()` (F18)
`packages/shared/package.json` has `./telemetry` subpath export (F13)
Interceptor errors are caught per-interceptor (fail-open); prototype pollution keys stripped from returned args (F09, F16)

docs.doc: Story specification with full type definitions, class designs, and bridge wiring patterns
Technical architecture (event sourcing, naming conventions, error handling patterns)
Project coding standards, TypeScript strict mode, async/await patterns, naming conventions

code.file: DiagnosticsEventType union, DiagnosticsErrorCode, DiagnosticsEventBase, ToolDiagnosticsEvent, ProviderDiagnosticsEvent discriminated union (F01)
DiagnosticsQueue class implementing IDiagnosticsQueue, DiagnosticsQueueLogger, droppedCount, structured error logging, re-drain dispose (F02, F05, F12, F17, F18)
createDiagnosticsProcessor() factory mapping events to UsageRecordInput
ToolInterceptorChain with per-interceptor error isolation and prototype pollution protection, IContentSanitizer/validateUrl factories (F09, F10, F11, F16)
Unit tests for ToolInterceptorChain and interceptor factories
Add interceptorChain field (via MCPClientOptions or setter), runPre/runPost in invokeTool() with warning logging (F04, F14)
AgentType union type used by DiagnosticsEvent
ICostTracker, UsageRecordInput, Provider, TaskType used by diagnostics processor
ILogger interface used by diagnostics processor

dependencies.dependency: Shared types package -- DiagnosticsQueue lives here
MCP client package -- ToolInterceptorChain lives here
Cost tracking -- ICostTracker interface consumed by diagnostics processor

constraints.constraint: DiagnosticsQueue lives in `@tamma/shared` with no dependency on `@tamma/mcp-client`; ToolInterceptorChain lives in `@tamma/mcp-client` with no queue or processor logic
`DiagnosticsQueue.emit()` must be synchronous -- no await, no Promise return, no blocking the caller
Drain timer must use `.unref()` so it does not keep the Node.js process alive
`drainPromise` guard must prevent concurrent drain from timer callback and `dispose()` racing
Processor errors must be logged via DiagnosticsQueueLogger.warn() -- they must not crash the queue or lose events (F05)
Interceptor errors must be caught per-interceptor; non-security interceptors fail-open, security-critical interceptors fail-closed (F09)
Interceptor return values must be filtered for forbidden keys (__proto__, constructor, prototype) (F16)
Tool arguments in DiagnosticsEvent must be truncated to MAX_DIAGNOSTICS_ARG_SIZE (10KB) (F06)
Sensitive keys (password, token, apiKey, secret, authorization) must be redacted from args; errorMessage must be sanitized (F07)
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode (strict: true, noImplicitAny, noImplicitReturns)
Files use kebab-case; interfaces use I-prefix; types use PascalCase; constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
Use `.js` extensions in import paths for ESM compatibility
Interceptors must not mutate input args or results -- return new objects
Interceptor chain preferably accepted via MCPClientOptions; setInterceptorChain() setter also supported (F04)

interface.name: ICostTracker
interface.description: Main cost tracker service interface with recordUsage(UsageRecordInput): Promise<UsageRecord>

interface.name: UsageRecordInput
interface.description: Input for creating a usage record: projectId, engineId, agentType, taskId, taskType, provider, model, inputTokens, outputTokens, totalTokens, latencyMs, success, errorCode?, traceId?, cacheReadTokens?, cacheWriteTokens?

interface.name: ILogger
interface.description: Logging interface with info(), warn(), error(), debug() methods and optional child() factory

interface.name: ToolResult
interface.description: Tool invocation result: { success: boolean; content: ToolResultContent[]; error?: string; metadata?: { latencyMs, serverName, toolName } }

interface.name: ToolInvocationOptions
interface.description: Tool invocation options: timeout?, signal?: AbortSignal

interface.name: IMCPClient
interface.description: MCP client interface with invokeTool(), listTools(), readResource(), on(), dispose()

interface.name: IDiagnosticsQueue
interface.description: Queue interface with emit(), setProcessor(), dispose(), getDroppedCount() (F18)

interface.name: DiagnosticsQueueLogger
interface.description: Logger interface for queue: warn(msg, context?), debug?(msg, context?) (F02)

interface.name: IContentSanitizer
interface.description: Content sanitizer with sanitize(content: string): { result: string; warnings: string[] } (F10)

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock external dependencies; no real API calls in unit tests

locations.location: Unit tests for ToolInterceptorChain and built-in interceptor factories

ideas.idea: Test emit() returns synchronously and does not trigger drain immediately
Test queue drains on interval (use fake timers)
Test drainPromise guard: call dispose() while timer drain is in-flight, verify no concurrent execution
Test processor throwing does not lose subsequent events
Test queue drops oldest when maxQueueSize is exceeded
Test dispose() stops timer and flushes all remaining events
Test timer uses .unref() by checking the return value of setInterval
Test createDiagnosticsProcessor maps tool:complete event to UsageRecordInput with correct field mapping
Test createDiagnosticsProcessor maps provider:complete event to UsageRecordInput
Test createDiagnosticsProcessor skips tool:invoke and provider:call events (non-completion)
Test createDiagnosticsProcessor handles missing optional fields with defaults (tokens=0, model='unknown')
Test pre-interceptor receives args, returns modified args
Test post-interceptor receives result, returns modified result
Test multiple pre-interceptors run in order, each receiving output of previous
Test multiple post-interceptors run in order, each receiving output of previous
Test empty ToolInterceptorChain.runPre() returns original args with no warnings
Test empty ToolInterceptorChain.runPost() returns original result with no warnings
Test createSanitizationInterceptor sanitizes text content in ToolResult
Test createUrlValidationInterceptor adds warning for blocked URLs
Test invokeTool with interceptor chain calls runPre before execution
Test invokeTool with interceptor chain calls runPost after execution
Test invokeTool without interceptor chain works unchanged (backward compat)
Test bridge: mcpClient tool:invoked event maps to diagnosticsQueue tool:invoke event
Test bridge: mcpClient tool:completed event with success=true maps to diagnosticsQueue tool:complete
Test bridge: mcpClient tool:completed event with success=false maps to diagnosticsQueue tool:error
Test getDroppedCount() returns correct count after queue overflow (F02)
Test drain() logs structured warning on processor failure via DiagnosticsQueueLogger (F05)
Test dispose() re-drains until queue is empty (F12)
Test events delivered in FIFO order within batch (F17)
Test interceptor error is caught and chain continues with unmodified args (F09)
Test prototype pollution keys are stripped from interceptor output (F16)
Test invokeTool logs pre/post interceptor warnings (F14)
Test bridge wiring includes correlationId in both start and end events (F15)
Test bridge wiring truncates args exceeding MAX_DIAGNOSTICS_ARG_SIZE (F06)
Test bridge wiring redacts sensitive keys from args (F07)
Test bridge wiring sanitizes errorMessage (F07)
Test createDiagnosticsProcessor uses mapProviderName/mapTaskType instead of unsafe casts (F08)
